content: Emergency treatment for K+ >6.0-6.5 mEq/L or ECG changes: (1) Cardiac membrane stabilization: calcium gluconate 1g IV (or calcium chloride 500mg via central line), repeat if ECG unchanged after 5-10 minutes; (2) Shift potassium intracellularly: regular insulin 10 units IV with 25g dextrose (onset 15-30 min, lasts 4-6 hours); albuterol 10-20mg nebulized (adjunctive); sodium bicarbonate 50-100 mEq IV if acidemic; (3) Remove potassium: loop diuretics (if renal function adequate), potassium binders (sodium polystyrene sulfonate 15-60g PO, patiromer, sodium zirconium cyclosilicate), hemodialysis (definitive for severe cases or renal failure). Chronic management: dietary potassium restriction, review/adjust medications causing hyperkalemia, chronic potassium binders for CKD patients on RAAS inhibitors, treat underlying cause. Monitor for rebound hypokalemia after treatment.
